name: KGS 1x1 VLS
description: Although the 9, 18, and 36 cell variants of the Avgi's Vertical Launch Array are by far the most common, their modular nature allows them to be easily made in all manner of odd sizes. This is the cell that all Avgi vertical launch systems are built from. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cell's contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This cell's negligible footprint makes it suitable for stuffing into nooks and crannies left over from other weapons. Sadly, the system still requires a turret mount. 	These tiny vertical launch cells enable versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 1x2 VLS
description: This extremely tiny vertical launch system contains two independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's extremely compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the four cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 2x2 VLS
description: This very tiny vertical launch system contains four independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's very compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the six cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 2x3 VLS
description: This tiny vertical launch system contains six independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it even more suitable for drones and fighters than the nine cell version, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 3x4 VLS
description: This tiny vertical launch system contains twelve independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it suitable for drones and fighters, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 3x5 VLS
description: This tiny vertical launch system contains fifteen independent launch cells, each occupying one ton of outfit space. Compatible with small-to-medium Avgi missiles, the cells' contents can be easily swapped to suit a given mission. This array's compact footprint makes it suitable for drones and fighters, though the system still requires a turret mount. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 4x6 VLS
description: This small vertical launch system contains 24 independent launch cells in a rectangular array. Designed for flexibility, the system can employ a variety of small or medium-sized missiles. Owing to its large surface footprint, a turret mount is required for the launcher to deploy missiles safely. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 5x6 VLS
description: This small vertical launch system contains 30 independent launch cells in a rectangular array. Designed for flexibility, the system can employ a variety of small or medium-sized missiles. Owing to its large surface footprint, a turret mount is required for the launcher to deploy missiles safely. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 6x8 VLS
description: This medium vertical launch system contains 48 independent launch cells arrayed in parallel. A wide variety of small to mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the launch cells, which can be easily interchanged. Designed for swift vertical cold-launch, the system needs an open space comparable to a turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 6x10 VLS
description: This medium vertical launch system contains 60 independent launch cells arrayed in parallel. A wide variety of small to mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the launch cells, which can be easily interchanged. Designed for swift vertical cold-launch, the system needs an open space comparable to a turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 6x12 VLS
description: This large vertical launch system contains 72 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. A variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the interchangeable launch cells. Designed for quick vertical launch, this system needs a turret mount for the required open space to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 9x12 VLS
description: This large vertical launch system contains 108 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. A variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles are compatible with the interchangeable launch cells. Designed for quick vertical launch, this system needs a turret mount for the required open space to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 12x12 VLS
description: This huge vertical launch system contains 144 independent launch cells in a rectangular formation. The interchangeable launch cells are compatible with a variety of small and mid-sized Avgi missiles. Designed for speedy vertical launch, this system requires a large turret mount to function properly. 	Vertical launch systems enable rapid and versatile missile deployment, but as dual launchers and magazines, no separate missile storage is available. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Twin Mandible Cannon
description: These two Mandible Cannons have been bolted together, creating a devastating long range weapon.

name: KGS Hyperpulse Cannon
description: The Hyperpulse Cannon is a more recent development of the Hai, a Pulse Cannon adapted to fire projectiles with much higher velocity, accuracy, and damage.

name: KGS Bipulse Ripper
description: The Unfettered have joined two Pulse Cannons, resulting in an effective and efficient weapon.

name: KGS Twin Railgun
description: The twin Skipper Railgun is favored by pilots who appreciate the ion and scrambling damage of the regular Skipper Railgun, but find it too small for use on ships larger than a drone. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Triple Railgun
description: This weapon is, quite simply, the internals of three Skipper Railguns in a larger frame. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Quad Railgun
description: Many captains have seen the power of the Skipper Railgun, and wanted a version fit for use on larger ships. This is the result - four Skipper Railguns in a larger housing. The recoil is intense. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 2x3 Railgun
description: The recoil from this weapon probably renders reverse thrusters unnecessary. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 3x3 Railgun
description: Only the most deranged of captains use these. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 3x4 Railgun
description: The few captains who use these swear that flying backwards with them make engines unnecessary. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 4x4 Railgun
description: The creator was drunk on acorn liquor when this was designed... 	Due to the huge fire rate, it was impossible to synchronize the firing of this weapon. As a result, it fires twice every shot. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 4x5 Railgun
description: Why not? 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Mini Blaster
description: The Energy Blaster has long been popular for its small size, allowing it to be stuffed almost anywhere. The Mini Blaster is an attempt to capitalize on this, featuring an even smaller footprint, but even more disappointing performance. 	As with the Energy Blaster, it is decently effective in large numbers.

name: KGS Particle Carronade
description: The Particle Carronade is a miniaturized Particle Cannon that fires smaller particle bolts, tuned for higher damage and a more rapid fire rate. Range and accuracy are reduced, but the small bolts allow for excellent velocity, and is thus a popular anti-fighter weapon.

name: KGS Particle Howitzer
description: The Particle Howitzer is a scaled-up Particle Cannon, firing larger particle bursts with higher velocity. It has better range and damage than the Particle Cannon, but fires more slowly, and is also much larger.

name: KGS Particle Burster
description: The pinnacle of pirate technology, the Particle Burster is a hodgepodge of two Particle Carronades and some Proton Gun components, among other various odds and ends. As its name suggests, it fires particle bolts in bursts, and is commonly found equipped on the flagships of various pirate warlords, for facing off with other gangs or the authorities. The bolts fired by this weapon are of lower accuracy and velocity than the Particle Carronade's, but are modified for more damage.

name: KGS Dual Particle Cannon
description: The Dual Particle Cannon is incredibly space-efficient for its firepower. However, it is less accurate, consumes more energy and creates quite a lot more heat than two Particle Cannons.

Ships: Cruiser (KGS Dual Particle Cannon Mark II)

name: KGS Plasma Assault Repeater
description: The new main weapon of the Kingfisher fast heavy warship, the Plasma Assault Repeater is a Plasma Repeater adapted to fire in bursts. The modifications resulted in slightly increased overall damage, at the cost of increased energy use and heat generation.

name: KGS Particle Turret
description: The Lovelace Particle Turret is a new product designed for the heavy anti-capital role, mounting two powerful Particle Cannons on a rotating base. It has also become popular as an anti-fighter weapon for its excellent range and projectile velocity.

name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod
description: A Javelin Mini Pod cut down to just a single tube, the Javelin Micro Pod is so small it is almost useless. However, it can fit nearly anywhere. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Javelin Launcher
description: This is simply two Javelin Pods welded together to fit in a single gun port. It is favored by captains who have sufficient space, but insufficient hardpoints to mount Javelin Pods. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Javelin Storm Pod
description: The Javelin Mini Pod has long been a favorite of fighter pilots for its high damage and fire rate. The Javelin Storm Pod is a pair of Javelin Mini Pods welded together, but with a twist: it fires its eight tubes very, very rapidly, dealing great burst damage. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Javelin Storm Launcher
description: The Javelin Storm Launcher, developed by Kraz Cybernetics for use on Tarazed's Kingfisher battlecruiser, fires all its 24 tubes very quickly, perfect for the Kingfisher's strike tactics. 	The weapon's extremely concentrated burst fire makes it almost impossible for anti-missile systems to counter all the missiles, ensuring that at least some of them hit. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

name: KGS Javelin Strike Pod
description: The Javelin Strike Pod is a Javelin Mini Pod adapted to fire modified Strike Javelins - regular Javelins with a much larger propellant section. 	Unfortunately, the systems that enable the three-shot burst fire on the full-size launcher couldn't fit on this pod. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Javelin Strike Launcher
description: The Javelin Strike Launcher is a Javelin Pod adapted to fire modified Strike Javelins - regular Javelins with a much larger propellant section. 	To maximize the chance of a hit at long range, this weapon has been adapted to fire in bursts of three. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer
description: The Javelin Pod's greatest strength is in its numbers, and thus this weapon exemplifies that further. Tiny Javelin Micro Pods, stripped down to the bare minimum loading magazine with small maneuvering thrusters for aiming correction, are launched in considerable number, providing a sustained fire rate equal to that of 6 Javelin Pods at the cost of massively increased tonnage overhead. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

name: KGS Twin Gatling
description: These two Gatling Guns have been crudely joined to fit into a single gun port, offering twice as much damage but twice the power consumption and heat, along with a little bit of extra mass. However, it still needs to cool down for twice as long as it fires, and can only sustain a maximum of three seconds of continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Staggered Twin Gatling
description: These two Gatling Guns have been crudely joined to fit into a single gun port, offering twice as much damage but twice the heat and power consumption, along with a little bit of extra mass. However, an intricate mechanism staggers the fire of the two guns. It needs to cool down for half as long as it fires, and can only sustain a maximum of six seconds of continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Triple Gatling
description: These three Gatling Guns have been joined rather more nicely than the pirate-made Twin Gatling. An ingenious device staggers the fire of all three guns, allowing for sustained continuous fire. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Quad Gatling
description: This scary weapon has been cobbled together from 4 Gatling Guns. Barmy Edward took inspiration from the Staggered Gatling Gun made and used by pirates, and this is the result - a weapon with huge damage potential, large enough to be used by capital ships. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS 2x3 Gatling
description: This absurd weapon was created by Barmy Edward. When asked why he designed it, he simply grins. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Quad Gatling Turret
description: A pirate warlord has had two Staggered Twin Gatling Guns attached to a turret mount. Needless to say, the result is a terror, with a much faster cooldown time in between bursts than the original Gatling Turret. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Gatling Snubnose
description: A creative pirate has chopped off most of the barrel of a Gatling Gun, resulting in a much lighter and faster firing weapon. However, the downsides are much diminished range, accuracy, and muzzle velocity, combined with a lower thermal limit and a hefty cooldown time. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS High Velocity Gatling Gun
description: A creative pirate has gone in the opposite direction of the Gatling Snubnose, and has instead lengthened the barrel of the Gatling Gun, resulting in higher muzzle velocity and accuracy. The longer barrel also results in a higher thermal limit and more effective cooling, allowing it to fire continuously, though at a lower rate because of the increased barrel weight. 	Ammunition for this weapon can be restocked at any outfitter.

name: KGS Twin Flamethrower
description: The already-fearsome Flamethrower has been made doubly scary with this twinned version. Two Flamethrowers have been joined in a fashion that allows for slightly better fuel efficiency at the cost of heat.

name: KGS Strike Javelin
description: The Strike Javelin missile is ammunition for the Javelin Strike Launcher; without a launcher to store them in you cannot purchase or use Strike Javelins.

name: KGS Strike Javelin Storage Crate
description: The Strike Javelin Storage Crate is used to store extra ammunition for Javelin Strike Launchers.

name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Package
description: This package is a stripped-down Javelin Micro Pod, with a minimized loading magazine holding 6 Javelins, and a few small chemical thrusters for aiming. The Javelin Micro Pod Package is ammunition for the Javelin Micro Pod Deployer; without a deployer to store them in you cannot purchase or use Javelin Micro Pod Packages.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

name: KGS Javelin Micro Pod Storage Rack
description: The Javelin Micro Pod Storage Rack is used to store extra ammunition for Javelin Micro Pod Deployers.

Ships: KGS Kingfisher (KGS Javelin Micro Pod Deployer)

name: KGS Binary Repeater
description: Two Thermal Repeaters have been wired together, resulting in rather insane heat output.

name: KGS Double Digger Mining Beam
description: These two Digger Mining Beams have been stacked. Some clever engineering allows it to be slightly more effective than two separate beams, at the cost of a requiring little more energy and making a little more heat.

name: KGS Double Digger Mining Turret
description: The complement to the Double Digger Mining Beam, this handy turret, originally meant for asteroid mining, is actually quite effective against ships due to its shield disruption capabilities.

name: KGS Twinhibitor
description: Simply two Inhibitor Cannons in a single frame, this weapon is favored by Remnant captains trying to fit the maximum number of the long-range, slowing weapons as possible on their ship.

name: KGS Bithrasher
description: The Bithrasher was created when several Remnant captains expressed dissatisfaction because filling their ships with Thrasher Cannons left a lot of weapon space free.

name: KGS Trithrasher
description: The Trithrasher is for captains who want even more than the Twin Thrasher. It does incredible damage up close, especially against hull.
